hero.question: Will Cameron Young win the 2026 Masters tournament?
hero.prob_take:
At 27.3%, the crowd is pricing Young as a top-10 favorite despite him ranking outside the PGA Tour's elite tier, which screams overvaluation relative to his actual Masters form and competing against 50+ players with better recent finishes.

The massive 24-hour spike on $4.2M volume suggests smart money piled in on something concrete (a recent hot streak, injury news on competitors, or betting shop action), so confirm what actually moved before fading this move.

daily_take.headline: Cameron Young's Masters odds just jumped 24 points. Here's why that's wrong.
daily_take.deck: Cameron Young popped from 3% to 27.3% in 24 hours on a single strong tournament result. The market overreacted. Young has 1 PGA win in his career and zero major championship experience. McIlroy's odds collapsed 35 points in tandem, which tells you this is pure recency bias, not new information. Fade the Young spike and buy McIlroy's dip.

sidebar.headline: McIlroy's -35pt move is mechanical selloff, not fundamental. World No. 3 still the Masters favorite by process of elimination.
